entries.title: About We follow Mason’s model and do clay modeling every year for one term. We are revamping our clay modeling program with a focus on one hand-building technique per year for younger and inexperienced students and more connections across the curriculum in observation and narration assignments. During the second term, students will learn how to do Sashiko work as they did in Japan and Redwork as was done in the United States and Europe. These are hand-stitching techniques that incorporate different stitches to create beautiful patterns. For the 3rd term, students will learn the art of cloisonné, which regained popularity in the West during that time. Cloisonne is an enameling technique that uses metal strips to create delicate designs.

These are hand-stitching techniques that incorporate different stitches to create beautiful patterns. For the 3rd term, students will learn the art of cloisonné, which regained popularity in the West during that time. Cloisonne is an enameling technique that uses metal strips to create delicate designs.
entries.content:
Handicrafts
Term 1: Clay Modeling
"In the modelling lessons there should be as little of the routine of ordinary lessons as possible

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: so that it may be regarded as a recreation and a pleasure

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and that each child may be free to do the work by the method he likes best

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and to realize the delight of exercising his creative power- a feeling which is strong in all children." A Manual of Clay-Modeling by Hermione Unwin

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
p.2
Students will explore the slab technique to create various projects. The focus is on fostering creativity

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: observation

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and manual dexterity. Teachers are encouraged to adapt lessons to suit students' needs and may choose to follow the videos closely or develop their own approach. Video tutorials are available; younger students and beginners may require adult supervision. It’s recommended to establish lesson structure

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: cleanup

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and storage procedures before starting. Teachers should also review the “Teacher Prep” and dry clay reworking tutorials in the master folder. Clay can be purchased as regular terracotta or buff clay

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
as opposed to air-dry clay but pieces must be fired in a kiln to keep.
 The overall purpose of the clay modelling lessons is to encourage observation

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: hand-eye coordination

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: manual dexterity

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: as well as develop higher order thinking processes such as the order of beauty

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: truth

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: and likeness. Teachers are encouraged to assign clay modeling throughout the curriculum to give students an opportunity to interact with knowledge in different ways. These assignments can be as simple as creating the characters in their literature or history lessons which will help them to visualize and solidify what they are learning. Mapwork is a great way to incorporate clay modeling into lessons. Also

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: students could build the structures they are studying in architecture or history. Creating models of tools

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
weapons or clothing are also great ways to interact with lessons. 

Term 2: Sashiko & Red Work
Sashiko is a traditional Japanese embroidery technique that uses simple stitches such as the running stitch to create decorative patterns. This technique uses specialized needles and thread that does not have a sheen like embroidery floss does. The designs are traditionally white thread on indigo fabric. It was the poorer class of Japanese that created and made popular this craft during the Edo period

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: as they used it to patch worn out fabrics in a beautiful way. However

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: the results were so attractive that when sashiko made a resurgence during the Meiji period

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: the more wealthy class began purchasing these creations and wearing them proudly. Around the same time red work became fashionable in the United States and throughout western countries. This is a similar style of embroidery that also uses simple stitches but leans into the outline stitches such as the back stem

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: chain and split stitch. These stitches are done with red embroidery thread on white or cream fabric. This was a way to beautifully decorate simple

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
everyday items. 
Students will learn about how various crafts and trends spread throughout the world

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: while being adapted regionally

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
due to higher levels of global trading and travel. These crafts go along with the history rotation for the 2026/2027 school year. Students will create a few projects in each craft and be challenged to create beautiful objects to share with others.

Term 3: Cloisonne
Cloisonne is an ancient art form where artists traditionally solder gold or silver wire to a base and fill it with fine ground glass enamel. Students will have an opportunity to practice a variation of this craft by gluing wire to a base and filling the spaces with fine colored sand. Students will start by learning to bend wire and glue it into preferred shapes and lines. They will progress from there with projects that will increase in difficulty. Younger students will need teacher supervision as the wire can be somewhat difficult to bend and the glue

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: though non toxic

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: needs to be controlled.
